subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: The research question is stated verbatim and drives every methodological choice, ensuring sustained focus.
A coherent two-pronged methodology (manual vs computational) is logically sequenced and justified with reference to the RQ.
Intermediate objectives (significant-figure benchmarks, integrand choice) demonstrate tight focusing on âefficiencyâ.
weaknesses: Occasional minor wording slips (e.g. âanalysis methodsâ vs âintegration methodsâ) introduce brief ambiguity.
The methodology relies heavily on tertiary sources (ChatGPT, YouTube tutorials) rather than scholarly literature.
Late clarification of the four significant-figure stopping criterion creates small moments of uncertainty.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation maintains very clear and sustained focus on the comparison of trapezoidal and midpoint efficiency. Every section ties back to the research question, and the methodological design is well explained and appropriate. Minor lapses in wording and source choice prevent a perfect 6 but place the work firmly in the upper part of the 5â6 band.

strengths: The student demonstrates excellent command of calculus and numerical-analysis concepts, deriving and generalising both rules from first principles.
Subject-specific terminology and notation (absolute/relative error, Newton-Cotes, summation notation) are used accurately and consistently.
Equations are well numbered and integrated into the narrative, and code comments employ correct computing vocabulary.
weaknesses: A few typographical errors and informal phrases (e.g. âbetter methodâ) interrupt theoretical precision.
Global error bounds and relative error treatment are only superficially addressed.
Isolated notational inconsistencies (e.g. Î± vs a, undefined function g) break the otherwise rigorous presentation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work shows strong and relevant application of subject knowledge with consistently accurate use of terminology and notation. Superficial treatment of formal error bounds and minor typographical issues prevent a flawless 6 but justify a solid 5 in the 5â6 band.

strengths: The investigation includes robust triangulation of data: manual calculations, a custom Python/Java program and graphical visualisations that directly address the RQ.
Analysis goes beyond description, quantifying efficiency via sub-interval counts and convergence charts, and interpreting anomalous results thoughtfully.
Conclusions are well supported, synthesising manual and computational findings and explicitly linking back to the research question.
weaknesses: The evaluation remains largely qualitative, with no formal uncertainty quantification or confidence intervals.
Critical appraisal of lower-credibility sources (ChatGPT, APIs) is brief and could be deepened.
Recommendations for further work are speculative and lack concrete methodological proposals.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Research quality and depth of analysis consistently meet the 7â9 band descriptors, with extensive data and clear interpretation. A more rigorous, quantitative evaluation of error sources and source reliability would be needed for the top of the band, so an overall score of 10 appropriately reflects very strong critical thinking with room for deeper critical evaluation.

strengths: The report follows a clear, appropriate structure with logical sequencing of title, introduction, methodology, analysis and conclusion.
Headings, sub-headings, tables, figures and code blocks are consistently captioned and cross-referenced to support reader understanding.
Equations and lists are well formatted, and the overall layout supports comprehension.
weaknesses: Page numbers are missing and some tables and figures lack complete axis labels.
Text is ragged-right in places, and font/spacing inconsistencies occur between code and narrative.
Figure numbering and caption detail are occasionally inconsistent or incomplete.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation fully meets the top-band descriptors for structure and formatting. Minor inconsistencies in page numbering, alignment and figure labelling prevent perfection but justify a full 4 in Presentation.

strandSelections.reasoning:
**Strengths**: The research question is presented verbatim on the title page and again in the introduction, is concise (comparison of trapezoidal vs midpoint efficiency) and drives the whole investigation.  All methodological choices, data collection and analysis are repeatedly justified with reference to this RQ.  Intermediate objectives (e.g. one-significant-figure hand work, four-significant-figure computational test) show tight focusing.

**Weaknesses**: Minor wording slips ("analysis methods" vs "integration methods") and the late mention of the 4 s.f. benchmark create small moments of ambiguity but never derail the line of inquiry.

Overall clarity and sustained focus place the work firmly in the top band (5â6).

**Strengths**: The analysis moves beyond description by: 
â¢ Quantifying efficiency via sub-interval counts and calculation tallies.
â¢ Comparing manual versus computational results and relating both to theoretical accuracy expectations.
â¢ Employing visualisations (line graphs) that illustrate convergence behaviour.
The student interprets anomalies (e.g., 29-sub-interval spike) and provides plausible explanations, demonstrating thoughtful engagement.

**Weaknesses**: Formal error propagation, statistical uncertainty or confidence intervals are absent, and the decision criteria for âefficiencyâ (first consistent 4 s.f. match) are somewhat ad hoc.  Discussion could probe sensitivity to function choice or alternative efficiency metrics.

These factors align with the 7â9 band, earning a solid 7 for depth of analysis.

**Strengths**: Mathematical terminologyâsub-interval, absolute/relative error, Newton-Cotes, Riemann sum, algorithm, APIâappears accurately and consistently.  Symbols are generally well defined, equations are numbered and referenced, and code comments employ correct computing vocabulary.

**Weaknesses**: Isolated inconsistencies (e.g., \alpha vs a, h mis-rounded in text, minor typographical errors in tables) interrupt the otherwise rigorous use of notation.  These are infrequent and do not impede understanding but preclude a flawless 6.

The strand meets the descriptors of the 5â6 band, meriting a score of 5.

**Strengths**: Word count and title are present; most tables and figures are captioned; equations are numbered; code is formatted in monospace blocks.  Improvements over the previous draft include corrected HTML artefacts and more consistent figure captions.

**Weaknesses**: Text remains ragged-right; some tables are mis-aligned; page numbers are still missing; a few axis labels are absent; font size and spacing vary slightly between code and narrative.  These issues indicate that layout considerations are only partly met.

Consequently, the strand fits the 1â2 band; a score of 2 is awarded.

**Strengths**: The essay demonstrates **excellent command of calculus and numerical-analysis concepts**.  The student derives the trapezoidal rule from first-order Lagrange interpolation, generalises to n sub-intervals, and similarly articulates the midpoint formula, showing confident manipulation of algebraic and integral expressions.  Programming knowledge is transferred appropriately to automate calculations and capture efficiency metrics.

**Weaknesses**: A few slips persist (e.g., typo in equation range, occasional informal phrasing about âbetter methodâ rather than mathematically precise efficiency definition; reliance on absolute error rather than relative error bars).  Some theoretical aspects such as global error bounds are mentioned only superficially.

Overall, subject knowledge is consistently and relevantly applied: top band achievement with a score of 5.

**Strengths**: The student states the topic, its significance and the comparative angle (hand vs computer) in the opening paragraph and revisits it throughout the report, ensuring that the reader is always aware of the purpose.  Background, derivations and contextual history are employed to frame the study, and every major section is explicitly linked to the central notion of âefficiencyâ.  Headings, tables and code excerpts repeatedly signal how each step feeds into answering the question.

**Weaknesses**: Occasional digressions (e.g. historical notes, API explanations) are lengthier than required and marginally blur the focus.  Nonetheless, these do not prevent a clear, accurate and purposeful communication of the topic overall.

Given the accurate, reader-friendly communication and sustained alignment with purpose, performance aligns with the 5â6 band.

**Strengths**: The essay follows the prescribed EE structure (title, abstract omitted but introduction substitutes, methodology, analysis, conclusion, bibliography).  Headings, sub-headings, tables, figures and code listings create a logical flow that supports reader comprehension.  Revisions have shortened paragraphs and ensured cross-referencing of most figures.

**Weaknesses**: Some sections (Background derivations) are disproportionately long relative to analysis; figure numbering is occasionally inconsistent; minor redundancy (duplicate image captions).  Nonetheless, the overall structure clearly supports understanding, satisfying the top band for Presentation.

Thus, the work earns 4 within the 3-4 band.

**Strengths**: The student gathers data from both manual calculations and a purpose-built Python/Java programme, triangulating findings with eight external sources and one relevant research paper.  The research consistently supports the RQ, and the student explains how each source or data-set informs the investigation.  Integration of graphical evidence (tables, charts) strengthens the argument.

**Weaknesses**: Peer-reviewed literature is still limited, and some sources (e.g., ChatGPT, YouTube tutorials) carry lower academic weight.  Critical appraisal of source reliability is brief.

Consequently, research quality sits comfortably in the 7â9 band but lacks the comprehensive, consistently high-quality source base for 10â12; a score of 7 is justified.

**Strengths**: The conclusion synthesises findings from both manual and computational strands, explicitly addressing the research question.  Limitations (calculator precision, alternative midpoint computation) are acknowledged and linked to potential effects on results.  The student contrasts initial hypothesis with results, showing reflection.

**Weaknesses**: Evaluation of methodology remains mostly qualitative; no quantitative assessment of error sources or robustness checks across different functions.  Recommendations for further work are present but somewhat speculative.

The argument is reasoned and partially critical, matching the 4â6 band; a score of 5 is appropriate.

**Strengths**: The student designs a two-pronged methodology (manual calculation and coded algorithm) that is well explained, logically sequenced and appropriate to the question.  Choice of a non-polynomial integrand, justification of interval selection, explanation of stopping criteria (1 s.f. and 4 s.f.) and inclusion of a calculation counter all illustrate **effective, informed selection** of methods and tools.

**Weaknesses**: The source base, though expanded, remains dominated by tertiary materials (websites, YouTube, ChatGPT).  Error analysis (e.g., absence of formal uncertainty or confidence bounds) and the rationale behind some numerical thresholds (e.g., why 500 as an upper limit) are only heuristically treated.  These limitations prevent the work from displaying the full completeness expected for a 6.

Performance is therefore best described by the upper part of the 5â6 band but not unequivocally exemplary; a score of 5 is appropriate.

annotations.comment: The composite rule formula (18) is expressed concisely and accurately in summation form, demonstrating effective use of notation and subject terminology.

The introduction succinctly communicates the topic, outlines both manual and computational approaches, and effectively links each methodological choice back to the research question and objective of comparing efficiency.
The phrase âanalysis methodsâ is used inconsistently; consider replacing with âintegration methodsâ to align precisely with the investigationâs focus and avoid momentary ambiguity.
The methodology cites ChatGPT and a non-peer-reviewed API tutorial, which have lower academic credibility. Incorporate more scholarly sources to strengthen evidence of informed selection.
Background derivations are very detailed but occupy a disproportionate amount of space. Consider summarizing historical context to maintain a sharper focus on efficiency analysis.
The table of contents is comprehensive and logically organized, providing clear signposting to all major sections and supporting reader orientation.
Figure 2.2.3 caption omits the number of rectangles and subintervals; enhance caption detail to fully describe the visualisation context.
The transition to the final trapezoidal formula (12) is executed impeccably, showing consistent and accurate use of subject-specific concepts and LaTeX equations.
The effective use of visualisations (trapezoidal figures) and algebraic derivations demonstrates a clear analysis strategy, illustrating convergence behaviour and supporting critical thinking.
Error analysis remains heuristic; the report would benefit from quantitative uncertainty estimates or confidence intervals to support the analysis depth and critical evaluation.
The derivation of the composite trapezoidal rule relies on a LibreTexts source. Incorporate a primary textbook or peer-reviewed derivation to strengthen research quality.
The choice of integrating f(x)=sin(xÂ²)/(x+1) over [0,â4Ï] is well justified: it ensures non-polynomial behavior and illustrates convergence characteristics effectively.
Section header formatting is inconsistent (extra blank line before <h2> tag). Ensure uniform heading styles throughout the report for professional presentation.

The research question is stated verbatim on the title page and again in the introduction, providing crystal-clear focus. This repeated statement effectively anchors the entire report to its core inquiry and ensures methodological choices directly align with the central comparison of efficiency.
Page numbers are not displayed in the footer. Adding sequential numbering will enhance navigation and meet standard academic presentation expectations.
Footnote cites Britannica as a tertiary source. For rigorous subject knowledge application, add peer-reviewed or primary academic references on the method of exhaustion.
The Lagrange interpolation formula is presented clearly in LaTeX, demonstrating strong command of mathematical notation and effective use of equations to derive the trapezoidal rule.
Word count is provided prominently on the title page, fully meeting IB formatting requirements and demonstrating attention to administrative presentation details.
The explanation of sign cancellations between equations (2) and (3) is informal and could be clarified with precise algebraic reasoning or a brief stepwise proof to reinforce theoretical rigor.
Discussion of the zero-value terms in equation (9) omits reference to formal global error bounds. Including an error-term derivation would deepen subject knowledge application.
The recognition of the coefficient pattern in equation (19) shows strong analytical insight and supports well-supported conclusions about rule generalisation.
Figure 2.2.1 image source is cited but the figure does not include a unit scale or axis labels. Ensure all figures include complete labels for clarity.
Midpoint rule formula (21) is correctly displayed in LaTeX and integrated within the narrative, reflecting consistent and precise use of mathematical notation.
Figure 2.1.3 lacks a clear y-axis label and tick marks. Complete axis annotations to improve readability and meet layout standards.
Notation for the midpoint height <math>g((x_{k+1}+x_k)/2)</math> appears without a definition of g. Define the approximate function explicitly to avoid confusion.
The general midpoint rule expression (24) lacks mention of its error term (O(hÂ²)). Including the local truncation error would demonstrate deeper theoretical understanding.
